Pooled 3-Cohort Neuropsych: Spontaneous Reversal (Class 1 Crude)

Minghui (Grace) Wang

2026-07-10

0. Overview

Method

For each test and sex stratum, observed change was calculated from baseline to the first follow-up and annualized by the elapsed follow-up time.

Class 1 thresholds were evaluated at each participant’s exact index age using fixed-effect predictions. For index age A and horizon h, the model contrast was:

Pred(age = A) - Pred(age = A - h)

For each horizon:

  • expected decline: threshold = -model_change;
  • expected improvement: threshold = 2 × model_change;
  • no expected change: threshold = 0.

Observed and model-estimated changes were sign-reversed for Trails A and Trails B so that positive values consistently indicate improvement.

Outputs include exclusive counts, cumulative counts, cumulative percentages, and participant-level scatter plots for baseline ages 50–90.

1. Setup

1.1 Output folders

SAMPLE <- "2visit"
covariates <- NULL
source("R/setup_analysis_sample.R")

model_tag <- if (SAMPLE == "2visit") "crude_2visit" else "crude"
dir_tag   <- if (SAMPLE == "2visit") "_2visit" else ""

class1_fits_file <- paste0(
  "pooled3_",
  model_tag,
  "_class1_fits.rds"
)

plot_dir     <- paste0(
  "../output/plots/pooled3_06_spontaneous_reversal_class1_crude", dir_tag
)
table_dir    <- paste0(
  "../output/tables/pooled3_06_spontaneous_reversal_class1_crude", dir_tag
)
dir.create(plot_dir,     recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(table_dir,    recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

1.2 Load saved Class 1 models

model_rds_dir <- "../output/models"

class1_fits <- readRDS(file.path(model_rds_dir, class1_fits_file))

class1_fits_success <- class1_fits |>
  filter(fit_success)

cat(
  "Loaded", nrow(class1_fits_success),
  "successfully fitted Class 1 model strata\n"
)
## Loaded 28 successfully fitted Class 1 model strata
class1_fits_success |>
  select(test, sex_label, n_obs, n_subjects, subject_random_slope_used) |>
  knitr::kable()
test sex_label n_obs n_subjects subject_random_slope_used
coding_correct Women 1800 874 TRUE
coding_correct Men 1599 775 TRUE
dsb Women 5683 2003 TRUE
dsb Men 4186 1553 TRUE
dsf Women 5789 2037 TRUE
dsf Men 4260 1573 TRUE
lmd Women 7244 2313 TRUE
lmd Men 5537 1863 TRUE

lmi Men 5570 1870 TRUE
lmr Women 6164 1975 TRUE
lmr Men 4715 1580 TRUE
pasd Women 6009 1936 TRUE
pasd Men 4612 1549 TRUE
pasi Women 6715 2115 TRUE
pasi Men 5063 1678 TRUE
pasr Women 3419 1428 TRUE
pasr Men 2663 1128 TRUE
trailsa Women 6109 2027 TRUE
trailsa Men 4925 1698 TRUE
trailsb Women 5861 1968 TRUE
trailsb Men 4769 1662 TRUE

vrd Men 5471 1848 TRUE
vri Women 7038 2275 TRUE
vri Men 5514 1858 TRUE
vrr Women 6702 2149 TRUE
vrr Men 5150 1708 TRUE

1.3 Test dictionary

test_dictionary <- make_pooled_test_dictionary(model_ready_pool)
test_dictionary |> knitr::kable()
test test_label xprize_subdomain range
dsf Digit Span Forward Attention 0-9
dsb Digit Span Backward Executive Function & Processing 0-8
trailsb Trail Making Test Part B Executive Function & Processing 0.32-10.00 minutes; maximum time set at 10 minutes
lmd Logical Memory, delayed recall Memory 0-23
lmi Logical Memory, immediate recall Memory 0-23
lmr Logical Memory, recognition Memory 0-11
pasd Paired Associates, delayed recall Memory 0-10
pasi Paired Associates, immediate recall Memory 0-21
pasr Paired Associates, recognition Memory 0-10
coding_correct WAIS-IV Coding, total correct Psychomotor 0-119
trailsa Trail Making Test Part A Psychomotor 0.32-10.00 minutes; maximum time set at 10 minutes
vrd Visual Reproductions, delayed recall Visual Learning 0-14
vri Visual Reproductions, immediate recall Visual Learning 0-14
vrr Visual Reproductions, recognition Visual Learning 0-14

1.4 Score direction

Trails A and Trails B are scored as completion time (higher = worse). For these tests, both observed and model changes are sign-flipped so that positive always means improvement.

test_dictionary |>
  mutate(
    score_direction = if_else(test %in% higher_is_worse_tests, -1L, 1L),
    direction_note  = if_else(
      score_direction == -1L,
      "Higher is worse (completion time); sign-flipped",
      "Higher is better"
    )
  ) |>
  select(test, test_label, score_direction, direction_note) |>
  knitr::kable()
test test_label score_direction direction_note
dsf Digit Span Forward 1 Higher is better
dsb Digit Span Backward 1 Higher is better
trailsb Trail Making Test Part B -1 Higher is worse (completion time); sign-flipped
lmd Logical Memory, delayed recall 1 Higher is better
lmi Logical Memory, immediate recall 1 Higher is better
lmr Logical Memory, recognition 1 Higher is better
pasd Paired Associates, delayed recall 1 Higher is better
pasi Paired Associates, immediate recall 1 Higher is better
pasr Paired Associates, recognition 1 Higher is better
coding_correct WAIS-IV Coding, total correct 1 Higher is better
trailsa Trail Making Test Part A -1 Higher is worse (completion time); sign-flipped
vrd Visual Reproductions, delayed recall 1 Higher is better
vri Visual Reproductions, immediate recall 1 Higher is better
vrr Visual Reproductions, recognition 1 Higher is better

2. Observed change (baseline to first follow-up)

Observed change was calculated from baseline to the first follow-up and annualized by the elapsed follow-up time. Records were restricted to index baseline ages 50–90 before calculating the model-based thresholds.

2.1 Compute per stratum

class1_with_obs <- class1_fits_success |>
  mutate(
    obs = map(
      data,
      \(data_x) {
        data_x |>
          compute_obs_change() |>                # defined in R/spontaneous_reversal_functions.R.
          restrict_reversal_baseline_age()       
      }
    )
  )

subject_obs <- class1_with_obs |>
  select(test, sex_label, obs) |>
  unnest(obs)

cat(
  "Observed change computed for",
  n_distinct(subject_obs$dbgap_subject_id),
  "unique subjects across",
  n_distinct(subject_obs$test), "tests\n"
)
## Observed change computed for 3495 unique subjects across 14 tests

2.2 Follow-up intervals

subject_obs |>
  group_by(test, sex_label) |>
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    median_fu_years = median(followup_years),
    iqr_lo = quantile(followup_years, 0.25),
    iqr_hi = quantile(followup_years, 0.75),
    max_fu_years = max(followup_years),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  knitr::kable(digits = 1)
test sex_label n median_fu_years iqr_lo iqr_hi max_fu_years
coding_correct Women 561 5.2 4.1 6.0 8.8
coding_correct Men 485 5.2 4.1 5.9 9.0
dsb Women 1630 5.3 2.4 7.0 20.3
dsb Men 1231 5.3 2.9 7.0 17.0
dsf Women 1662 5.3 2.4 7.0 20.3
dsf Men 1252 5.3 2.9 7.0 17.0
lmd Women 1850 5.8 3.4 6.9 19.0
lmd Men 1468 5.9 3.9 6.8 17.1
lmi Women 1859 5.8 3.4 6.9 20.3
lmi Men 1477 5.9 3.8 6.8 17.1
lmr Women 1574 5.8 3.1 6.8 19.0
lmr Men 1266 5.9 4.0 6.8 17.1
pasd Women 1542 5.8 3.2 6.8 19.0
pasd Men 1241 5.9 4.1 6.8 17.1
pasi Women 1744 5.8 3.3 6.9 19.0
pasi Men 1382 5.9 3.8 6.8 17.4
pasr Women 1139 5.9 3.6 7.2 13.1
pasr Men 898 5.8 4.3 7.2 12.0
trailsa Women 1567 5.9 3.6 6.8 19.0
trailsa Men 1301 5.9 4.1 6.8 18.0
trailsb Women 1526 5.9 3.6 6.9 19.0
trailsb Men 1275 5.9 4.1 6.8 18.0
vrd Women 1803 5.9 3.5 6.9 19.0
vrd Men 1450 5.9 3.9 6.8 17.1
vri Women 1816 5.9 3.5 6.9 19.0
vri Men 1460 5.9 3.9 6.8 17.1
vrr Women 1747 5.8 3.4 6.9 19.0
vrr Men 1393 5.9 3.9 6.8 17.1

3. Model-estimated changes (Class 1, exact age)

Class 1 model changes were evaluated directly from the fitted fixed effects at each participant’s exact index age. The backward-age contrast follows the definition in the Method section.

3.1 Compute per stratum

class1_with_mc <- class1_with_obs |>
  mutate(
    model_changes = pmap(
      list(model, obs),
      function(model_x, obs_x) {
        compute_model_change_class1(model_x, unique(obs_x$baseline_age))
      }
    )
  )

4. Reversal classification

Observed changes were joined to the exact-age Class 1 contrasts before applying the horizon-specific reversal thresholds.

4.1 Classify

reversal_subject_level <- class1_with_mc |>
  mutate(
    classified = pmap(
      list(obs, model_changes, test, sex_label),
      function(obs_x, mc_x, test_x, sex_x) {
        obs_x |>
          left_join(mc_x, by = "baseline_age") |>
          mutate(test = test_x, sex_label = sex_x) |>
          classify_reversal()
      }
    )
  ) |>
  select(classified) |>
  unnest(classified) |>
  restrict_reversal_baseline_age()

cat(
  "Classified", nrow(reversal_subject_level),
  "participant-test combinations (baseline age 50-90) across",
  n_distinct(reversal_subject_level$test), "tests\n"
)
## Classified 39599 participant-test combinations (baseline age 50-90) across 14 tests

4.2 Reversal summary

reversal_subject_level |>
  group_by(test, sex_label) |>
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    n_any_rev = sum(max_reversal_year > 0),
    pct_any = 100 * mean(max_reversal_year > 0),
    n_rev_5  = sum(reversal_5yr),
    n_rev_10 = sum(reversal_10yr),
    n_rev_15 = sum(reversal_15yr),
    n_rev_20 = sum(reversal_20yr),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  knitr::kable(digits = 1)
test sex_label n n_any_rev pct_any n_rev_5 n_rev_10 n_rev_15 n_rev_20

coding_correct Men 485 19 3.9 19 6 3 5

dsb Men 1231 313 25.4 313 273 244 232

dsf Men 1252 280 22.4 280 240 219 205

lmi Men 1477 372 25.2 352 204 141 111

pasi Women 1744 439 25.2 404 290 206 146

pasr Men 898 56 6.2 56 44 32 28
trailsa Women 1567 36 2.3 36 18 6 2
trailsa Men 1301 37 2.8 37 10 3 3

5. Table 4 outputs

Participant-level spontaneous reversal classifications were summarized by baseline-age group in three formats:

  • Exclusive count: maximum qualifying horizon;
  • Cumulative count: maximum qualifying horizon at or above each column;
  • Cumulative percentage: cumulative count divided by N.

5.1 Table 4 (exclusive form)

table4_exclusive <- make_table4(
  reversal_subject_level, test_dictionary, form = "exclusive"
)

table4_exclusive |>
  filter(test == "lmd") |>
  select(-xprize_subdomain, -test, -test_label, -age_bin) |>
  knitr::kable()
sex_label age_label N N reversal 5yr N reversal 10yr N reversal 15yr N reversal 20yr
Women 50-54 342 20 2 0 0
Women 55-59 334 56 19 0 2
Women 60-64 251 69 34 9 2
Women 65-69 224 0 22 46 13
Women 70-74 201 2 0 0 44
Women 75-79 176 6 2 0 9
Women 80-84 186 11 13 1 13
Women 85-90 136 9 6 1 5
Men 50-54 277 20 2 0 0
Men 55-59 299 48 5 0 0
Men 60-64 220 63 26 4 0
Men 65-69 209 4 41 29 12
Men 70-74 203 0 0 8 52
Men 75-79 133 7 0 0 24
Men 80-84 81 3 2 1 11
Men 85-90 46 5 2 0 8 
# write_table4_workbook() is defined in workbook_writers.R
# make_output_name() is defined in helpers.R.

write_csv(
  table4_exclusive,
  file.path(
    table_dir,
    make_output_name(
      paste0("xprize_pooled3_", model_tag, "_class1_table4_exclusive"),
      "csv"
    )
  )
)

table4_excl_xlsx <- file.path(
  table_dir,
  make_output_name(
    paste0("xprize_pooled3_", model_tag, "_class1_table4_exclusive"),
    "xlsx"
  )
)
write_table4_workbook(table4_exclusive, table4_excl_xlsx, form = "exclusive")

table4_cumulative <- make_table4(
  reversal_subject_level, test_dictionary, form = "cumulative"
)

# Preview one test.
table4_cumulative |>
  filter(test == "lmd") |>
  select(-xprize_subdomain, -test, -test_label, -age_bin) |>

    table_dir,
    make_output_name(

)

table4_cumul_xlsx <- file.path(
  table_dir,
  make_output_name(
    paste0("xprize_pooled3_", model_tag, "_class1_table4_cumulative"),
    "xlsx"
  )
)
write_table4_workbook(table4_cumulative, table4_cumul_xlsx, form = "cumulative")

5.3 Table 4 (cumulative percentage)

table4_cumulative_pct <- make_table4(
  reversal_subject_level, test_dictionary, form = "cumulative_pct"
)

# Preview one test (values are proportions of N).
table4_cumulative_pct |>
  filter(test == "lmd") |>
  select(-xprize_subdomain, -test, -test_label, -age_bin) |>
  knitr::kable(digits = 3)

write_csv(
  table4_cumulative_pct,
  file.path(
    table_dir,
    make_output_name(
      paste0("xprize_pooled3_", model_tag, "_class1_table4_cumulative_pct"),
      "csv"
    )
  )
)

table4_cumul_pct_xlsx <- file.path(
  table_dir,
  make_output_name(
    paste0("xprize_pooled3_", model_tag, "_class1_table4_cumulative_pct"),
    "xlsx"
  )
)
write_table4_workbook(
  table4_cumulative_pct, table4_cumul_pct_xlsx, form = "cumulative_pct"
)

6. Scatter plots

Scatter plots display the observations underlying Table 4. The x-axis shows index baseline age, and the y-axis shows direction-adjusted annualized observed change. Points are colored by maximum qualifying reversal horizon and panels are separated by sex.

6.1 Check one test

plot_reversal_scatter(reversal_subject_level, test_dictionary, "lmd")

6.2 Save all tests

scatter_order <- reversal_subject_level |>
  distinct(test) |>
  left_join(
    test_dictionary |> select(test, xprize_subdomain),
    by = "test"
  ) |>
  arrange(xprize_subdomain, test)

walk2(
  scatter_order$xprize_subdomain,
  scatter_order$test,
  function(subdomain_x, test_x) {

    p <- plot_reversal_scatter(
      reversal_subject_level, test_dictionary, test_x
    )

    subdomain_file <- subdomain_x |>
      str_to_lower() |>
      str_replace_all("&", "and") |>
      str_replace_all("[^a-z0-9]+", "_") |>
      str_replace_all("_+", "_") |>
      str_replace_all("^_|_$", "")

    test_file <- test_x |>
      str_to_lower() |>
      str_replace_all("[^a-z0-9]+", "_") |>
      str_replace_all("_+", "_") |>
      str_replace_all("^_|_$", "")

    ggsave(
      filename = file.path(
        plot_dir,
        paste0(
          "class1_reversal_scatter_",
          subdomain_file, "_", test_file,
          "_", output_suffix, ".pdf"
        )
      ),
      plot = p,
      width = 12,
      height = 6
    )
  }
)

cat("Scatter plots saved to:", plot_dir, "\n")
## Scatter plots saved to: ../output/plots/pooled3_06_spontaneous_reversal_class1_crude_2visit
